overview: The Marathon County HR, Finance, and Property Committee conducted routine business including disallowing an insurance claim, approving revised property values for auction, approving carry forward funds resolution, and amending the capital assets threshold policy. The committee also received introductions from new healthcare consultants and detailed financial updates on 2025 year-end and 2026 budget status.
committee: Marathon County HR, Finance, and Property Committee

agenda.item: Revised property values for public auction
Resolution to approve carry forward funds
Resolution to amend capital assets threshold policy
Introduction of healthcare consultants - National Insurance Services

discussions.body: Corporation Counsel presented a claim received December 5th from Mercedes Holmes relating to the death of her 3-year-old child Zalen Bernett, who was placed in a treatment foster care home licensed through another agency in Dunn County. The death was determined to be natural causes with no wrongdoing found through law enforcement and social service investigations. Outside counsel recommended formal disallowance citing no Marathon County liability. Chair Gibbs moved to disallow the claim per insurance carrier recommendation. Motion carried.

discussions.item: Revised property values for public auction
discussions.body: Staff reported two parcels on Mullen Street and South Third Avenue failed to sell twice on Wisconsin Surplus with bids not reaching appraised values. Per county ordinance, staff requested revaluation to $9,000 for 529 Mullen Street and $7,500 for 738 South Third Avenue. Chair Gibbs moved to approve revised values. Motion carried unanimously. Committee Chair Robinson asked about bidders who didn't pay being marked as non-pay and banned from future auctions.

discussions.item: Resolution to approve carry forward funds
discussions.body: Sam from Finance presented Resolution R2026 for program revenues and multi-year projects requiring fund carryovers. Notable items included veterans relief fund replenishment using 2025 budget excess to provide approximately three years of funding, and $142,731 for administration special projects including $75,000 for homelessness contract. Vice Chair Marshall questioned the register of deeds redacted records fund; staff agreed to research its purpose and potential repurposing. Chair Gibbs moved approval. Motion carried.

discussions.item: Resolution to amend capital assets threshold policy
discussions.body: Sam explained the capital assets threshold has been $5,000 since 2006 per GFOA guidance. The resolution proposes increasing the threshold from $5,000 to $10,000, consistent with a 2022 review that was never completed. Supervisor Hart moved approval to forward to full county board. Motion carried unanimously.

discussions.item: Introduction of healthcare consultants - National Insurance Services
discussions.body: HR Director Candace introduced National Insurance Services (NIS), awarded the healthcare consulting RFP. Representatives introduced their team specializing in public sector benefits consulting. They outlined focus areas including evaluating the near-site ATA clinic for return on investment, evaluating fully insured vs. self-insured funding models, and improving communication with the committee. Vice Chair Marshall asked about comparative costs and strategies to reduce emergency room usage; NIS noted virtual Teladoc services and clinic utilization as cost mitigation tools. Chair Gibbs discussed risk tolerance considerations for self-insurance.

Finance Director Sam provided comprehensive department-by-department review of unaudited 2025 financials. Key items included: county received $257,238 TID closure check from City of Wausau; $222,752 in unclaimed state property as one-time revenue; opioid fund cash at $2.2 million with $3.49 million in future settlements expected; parks ice revenue up $70,000. When asked about projected fund balance surplus, Sam indicated she would provide that at next meeting after capital assets reconciliation, but noted it 'won't be zero.'

Sam reported the finance team is fully staffed since mid-December with a new payroll analyst. The department conducted quarterly closeout training, report training, and countywide finance staff meetings. Year-end work included W-2 processing addressing the 'big beautiful bill' no-tax-on-overtime calculations, 1099s, and random cash audits. First quarter 2026 closeout targeted for May 31st with monthly closeouts thereafter. Administrator Lance praised Sam's leadership through W-2 challenges and noted upcoming recommendation to increase Social Services reserve account above current $400,000.

Treasurer Connie reported on tax collection activities including processing 1,582 delinquent tax notice letters in March (down from 1,786 last year). She discussed ongoing challenges with municipal treasurers making receiving errors, particularly around lottery credits. The office is working with Department of Revenue on training materials. Supervisor Lemmer asked about poverty trends; Connie noted payment agreements are no longer offered as most defaulted. Administrator Lance clarified eviction hearings referenced are part of county tax deed process, not general evictions, and emphasized acceleration of tax deed process protects homeowner equity.

actionItems: Approved revised property values of $9,000 for 529 Mullen Street and $7,500 for 738 South Third Avenue for public auction
Approved Resolution R2026 for carry forward funds to 2026 budget
Approved capital assets threshold policy amendment increasing threshold from $5,000 to $10,000 for county board consideration
Finance to research register of deeds redacted records fund purpose and potential repurposing
Finance to provide fund balance surplus projection at next meeting
Administrator to bring recommendation to increase Social Services reserve account above $400,000
NIS healthcare consultants to provide updates prior to budget assumptions development in early summer
Treasurer's office to work with DOR on municipal treasurer training for lottery credits
Next committee meeting scheduled for April 8th
title: Marathon County HR, Finance, and Property Committee Meeting
